resources.name: jmdict-eng
resources.provenance: scriptin/jmdict-simplified release asset; local extracted file hash pinned

resources.name: jpdb-frequency-kana
resources.provenance: JPDB v2.2 frequency dictionary by Kuuuube; not checked in — download separately and extract to Resources/

resources.name: wordfreq
resources.provenance: Python package; install with: pip install wordfreq mecab-python3 ipadic

resources.name: kanjidic2-all
resources.provenance: scriptin/jmdict-simplified kanjidic2-all release asset; download, extract, and rename to kanjidic2-all.json in Resources/

resources.name: pitch-accent
resources.provenance: Derived from UniDic kana-accent lex.csv via scripts/unidic_to_pitch_tsv.py; source archive: https://clrd.ninjal.ac.jp/unidic_archive/cwj/2.1.2/unidic-mecab_kana-accent-2.1.2_src.zip (sha256: 70793cacda81b403eda71736cc180f3144303623755a612b13e1dffeb6554591)

resources.name: sentence-pairs
resources.provenance: Built from Tatoeba export: 'Sentence pairs in Japanese-English - 2026-01-20.tsv' and 'Sentence pairs in English-Japanese - 2026-01-20.tsv' via scripts/build_sentence_pairs_tsv.py

resources.name: jlpt-vocab
resources.provenance: JLPT vocabulary level lists (N5-N1) by Jonathan Waller, https://www.tanos.co.uk/jlpt/ — licensed CC BY. UNOFFICIAL community estimates; no official JLPT vocab lists exist post-2010. Built into Resources/jlpt-vocab.tsv (columns: surface, reading, level; level = N-number, 5=N5 easiest .. 1=N1 hardest) by scripts/build_jlpt_vocab_tsv.py from the cleaned CSV mirror at https://github.com/Bluskyo/JLPT_Vocabulary (MIT), file data/vocab/results/JLPT_vocab_ALL.csv. Consumed by generate_db.import_jlpt_levels to populate the entry_jlpt_level table.

resources.name: radkfile2
resources.provenance: EDRDG RADKFILE2 (radical→kanji index, extended radical set). License: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Electronic Dictionary Research and Development Group). Download, gunzip, convert from EUC-JP to UTF-8 and save as Resources/radkfile2.utf8. Example: curl -sL http://ftp.edrdg.org/pub/Nihongo/kradzip.zip | gunzip | iconv -f EUC-JP -t UTF-8 > Resources/radkfile2.utf8

resources.name: kradfile2
resources.provenance: EDRDG KRADFILE2 (kanji→radical index, extended radical set). License: CC BY-SA 4.0. Download, gunzip, convert from EUC-JP to UTF-8 and save as Resources/kradfile2.utf8. Example: curl -sL http://ftp.edrdg.org/pub/Nihongo/kradzip.zip | gunzip | iconv -f EUC-JP -t UTF-8 > Resources/kradfile2.utf8

resources.name: handwriting-ja
resources.provenance: Tegaki-Zinnia Japanese handwriting recognition model (~25MB). Consumed by the Zinnia recognizer via the Packages/zinnia-swift local Swift package (feat/handwriting branch). License: LGPL — see the Tegaki project for redistribution terms. Download the Japanese model from https://tegaki.github.io and save as Resources/handwriting-ja.model.

resources.name: kanjivg
resources.provenance: KanjiVG (Kanji Vector Graphics) by Ulrich Apel et al. License: CC BY-SA 3.0. Single-file XML distribution. Download, unzip, and save as Resources/kanjivg.xml. Example: curl -sL https://github.com/KanjiVG/kanjivg/releases/download/r20220427/kanjivg-20220427-main.zip -o /tmp/kvg.zip && unzip -p /tmp/kvg.zip 'kanjivg-20220427.xml' > Resources/kanjivg.xml

resources.name: dictionary
resources.provenance: generated by generate_db.py from jmdict-eng, extras, jpdb-frequency-kana, wordfreq, kanjidic2-all, pitch-accent, and sentence-pairs
